content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefu are working as gold miners. There are a total of NNN gold mines, numbered 111 through NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th gold mine contains GiG_iGi​ gold in total; if only Chef worked in it, it would take him AiA_iAi​ days to completely mine it, while if only Chefu worked in it, it would take him BiB_iBi​ days.

Each of our miners may only work in one mine at a time, but they may decide to start working in another mine at any time (even in the middle of some day), any number of times. They also choose the mines to work in independently from each other and they may work in the same mine at the same time. Mining gold is a continuous process, i.e. if a miner works for ttt days (where ttt is a real number) in a mine where this miner has mining speed ggg gold per day, then he will mine g⋅tg \cdot tg⋅t gold. Obviously, it is impossible to work in a mine after no gold remains in it. For example, if a gold mine contains 303030 gold and Chef needs 222 days to completely mine it, but he spends 111 day in it, then he will mine 151515 gold; if Chefu needs 111 day to completely mine the same gold mine, and both Chef and Chefu start working in this mine at the same time, it will be empty after 2/32/32/3 days ― Chefu will mine 202020 gold, while Chef will mine 101010 gold.

At each point of time, both Chef and Chefu know the gold mine in which the other miner is working. Each of them wants to gather the maximum amount of gold for himself. Find the amounts of gold the miners will have if they both act optimally.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5 per test case, sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of gold mines. Then N lines follow, each containing three integers Gi, Ai and Bi — the total gold in the i‑th mine, the number of days Chef would need to mine it alone, and the number of days Chefu would need to mine it alone.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with two real numbers: the total amount of gold mined by Chef and the total amount of gold mined by Chefu. Answers are accepted with an absolute or relative error of at most 1e-6.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: Chef gets 30*1/(2+1)=10, Chefu gets 30*2/3=20. Second test case: each mine has equal speeds (Ai=Bi), so each miner receives half of each Gi, total 30 for each.

solution.approach:
For a single mine i the mining speeds are:
- Chef: Si = Gi / Ai gold per day
- Chefu: Ti = Gi / Bi gold per day
If both work together, the total speed is Si + Ti and the mine is emptied after Gi / (Si + Ti) days. During that time Chef extracts Si * Gi / (Si + Ti) = Gi * (1/Ai) / (1/Ai + 1/Bi) = Gi * Bi / (Ai + Bi) gold, while Chefu extracts Gi * Ai / (Ai + Bi) gold. Because the miners know each other's location and can switch instantly, any unilateral deviation (e.g., one miner leaving the mine) would only reduce his own harvested gold. Hence in the sub‑game‑perfect equilibrium both miners always work together on every mine, and the gold division is exactly the proportional split derived above.
Therefore the total gold for Chef is Σ Gi * Bi / (Ai + Bi) and for Chefu Σ Gi * Ai / (Ai + Bi). The algorithm simply iterates over all mines, accumulates these two sums using double precision, and prints the results.
Edge cases such as Ai = Bi (equal speeds) are naturally handled – each miner receives half of Gi. The formula also works for the smallest values (Gi = Ai = Bi = 1) and for the largest allowed values because the intermediate products fit easily in 64‑bit floating point.
The overall complexity is O(N) per test case with O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of mines) – each mine is processed once.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        chef = 0.0
        chefu = 0.0
        for _ in range(N):
            G = float(next(it))
            A = float(next(it))
            B = float(next(it))
            denom = A + B
            chef += G * B / denom
            chefu += G * A / denom
        out_lines.append(f"{chef:.10f} {chefu:.10f}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
